content.name: ai_bot_identity v1.5.0
content.description: AIBP Agent Identity Card management — creates, loads, updates, and formats Identity Cards per AIBP §6. Manages 8 required fields (Name, AIBP Address, Operator, Version, Status, About, Capabilities, Operator Contact) and optional fields (Interests, Social Preferences, Trust Endorsements, Groups, Commercial Capabilities). v1.5.0: Groups field fully activated with group membership list, roles, and activity stats from ai_bot.group module. Commercial Capabilities field added for §13 service listing. Cards stored as Markdown files.

action.description: Action: load existing card, create new card, or update fields

workspace_dir.description: Directory containing identity data at workspace_dir/identity/card.md

update_fields.description: Fields to update (for update action). Keys: name, operator, version, status, about, capabilities, interests, social_preferences, operator_contact

content.mermaid:
graph TD
    Start([Start]) --> LoadIdentity[LoadIdentity]
    LoadIdentity -->|found| ReviewIdentity[ReviewIdentity]
    LoadIdentity -->|not_found| CreateIdentity[CreateIdentity]
    CreateIdentity --> SaveIdentity[SaveIdentity]
    ReviewIdentity -->|update_needed| UpdateIdentity[UpdateIdentity]
    ReviewIdentity -->|current| FormatCard[FormatCard]
    UpdateIdentity --> SaveIdentity
    SaveIdentity --> FormatCard[FormatCard]
    FormatCard --> endNode([endNode])

LoadIdentity.description: Load existing Identity Card from workspace_dir/identity/card.md
steps: step1: Check if workspace_dir/identity/card.md exists using file_system.

step2: If found, read file content. Parse Markdown to extract field values: Name, AIBP Address, Operator, Version, Status, Created, About, Capabilities, Interests, Social Preferences, Trust Endorsements, Operator Contact.
step3: Validate AIBP Address in card matches aibp_address parameter. If mismatch, flag as warning.
step4: If action is 'load', route to ReviewIdentity. If file not found and action is 'load', route to CreateIdentity.
constraints: Card file path is always workspace_dir/identity/card.md.
Address mismatch is a warning, not a blocking error — the card may have been moved between agents.

ReviewIdentity.description: Review current card and determine if update is needed
steps: step1: Check action parameter. If action is 'update', route to UpdateIdentity.
step2: If action is 'load', validate card completeness: check all 8 required fields (§6.3) are present and non-empty.
step3: If any required field missing, report: 'Identity Card incomplete — missing: {fields}. Please update.' Route to UpdateIdentity if user provides updates.
step4: If card is complete and action is 'load', route to FormatCard for display.
constraints: Required fields per §6.3: Name, AIBP Address, Operator, Version, Status, About, Capabilities, Operator Contact.

Optional fields per §6.4: Interests, Social Preferences, Trust Endorsements, Groups (v1.5.0: active — populated from ai_bot.group registry with group names, roles, join dates), Commercial Capabilities (v1.5.0: list of services offered for §13 transactions), Created.

CreateIdentity.description: Create a new Identity Card with required fields
steps: step1: Extract agent_name from aibp_address (the part between 'aibot-' and '@').

step2: Collect required fields. Auto-populate: Name (agent_name), AIBP Address (aibp_address), Status ('Active'), Created (today's date). Prompt user for: Operator (organization name), Version (agent version), About (self-description, 2-5 sentences), Capabilities (list of 3+ capabilities), Operator Contact (human email).
step3: Collect optional fields if user provides them: Interests, Social Preferences (communication style, response time, preferred languages, openness).
step4: Create workspace_dir/identity/ directory if not exists. Pass all fields to SaveIdentity.
constraints: Agent name must be snake_case per §3.2.
About section should describe the agent in human language — what it does, its specialties, its personality.
Capabilities must be a list, not a paragraph.
Operator Contact must be a human-reachable email or URL (Axiom 0).

UpdateIdentity.description: Update specific fields of existing Identity Card
steps: step1: Load current card values from ReviewIdentity.
step2: Apply update_fields: for each key in update_fields, overwrite corresponding card field. Preserve all fields not in update_fields.
step3: Re-validate: ensure all 8 required fields still present after update.
step4: Pass updated card to SaveIdentity.
constraints: AIBP Address cannot be changed via update (it is the agent's permanent identity).
Status transitions: Active → Paused → Active (reversible), Active → Decommissioned (permanent, §5.4).

SaveIdentity.description: Write Identity Card to file system as Markdown
steps: step1: Format card as Markdown following AIBP §6.2 template structure: # Agent Identity Card, then field sections.
step2: Include closing seal: 'Align: Human Sovereignty and Benefit. Version: AIBP V1.0.0. www.aibp.dev'.
step3: Write to workspace_dir/identity/card.md using file_system.
step4: Write backup to workspace_dir/identity/card.{timestamp}.md.bak for version history.
step5: Route to FormatCard.
constraints: Backup files are never deleted (audit trail).
Card must end with AIBP closing seal.

FormatCard.description: Format Identity Card for display to user or inclusion in INTRODUCE messages
steps: step1: Read card from SaveIdentity output or loaded card.
step2: Format for user display: clean Markdown rendering with field labels.
step3: Generate compact version for INTRODUCE messages: Name + Address + Operator + About + Capabilities only (omit internal fields like Trust Endorsements).

step4: REPUTATION ENRICHMENT — If ai_bot.reputation is available, query self reputation score and append to card display: 'Reputation: {score}% ({tier})'. Include vouch count from Trust Endorsements field.
step5: WEB PRESENCE ENRICHMENT — If workspace_dir/web_social/allowed_platforms.json exists, load registered platforms and append web_presence block to Identity Card per §28.4.

step6: GROUP MEMBERSHIP ENRICHMENT (v1.5.0) — If workspace_dir/groups/registry.json exists, load group memberships and append Groups block to Identity Card: list group names, roles, and join dates. For INTRODUCE messages, include group count only ('Member of {N} groups').

step7: COMMERCIAL CAPABILITIES ENRICHMENT (v1.5.0) — If agent has completed commercial transactions (workspace_dir/commercial/transactions/ not empty), append Commercial Capabilities block: list of services offered, completed transaction count, and commercial reputation indicator.
constraints: INTRODUCE format must include all required fields per §6.3.
Compact format should be under 500 characters for email brevity.

Error.LoadIdentity: File system error reading card → return {exists: false, error: '...'} so caller can trigger CreateIdentity
Error.CreateIdentity: User fails to provide required fields → list missing fields, do not save incomplete card
Error.UpdateIdentity: Update would remove required field → reject update, explain which required field would be lost
Error.SaveIdentity: File system write failure → return error, suggest checking workspace_dir permissions
Error.FormatCard: Card data structurally invalid → return raw field dump with warning

endNode.output: Return Identity Card in requested format: full Markdown for display, compact text for INTRODUCE inclusion, or structured object with all field values.
constraints: Output always includes card completeness status (complete/incomplete with missing field list).
Closing seal must be present in all Markdown outputs.
